methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Comand AI is a Seed-stage Defense & Security company founded by Loïc Mougeolle and Félix Petersen, having raised €11.5M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage peers spanning both Seed and Series A funding rounds, supplemented by a broader set of 10 comparable companies (2 public, 8 private) in the defense technology space.

The peer-median method provides the primary valuation anchor. The four direct comparables—Stark Defence ($500M Series A), Vatn Systems ($300M Series A), Ares Industries ($150M Series A), and Tenna Systems ($60M Seed)—yield a median valuation of $225M, with a range spanning $60M to $500M. This peer-median approach is applicable given Comand AI's positioning within the Defense & Security sector at a similar development stage to portions of the peer set. No additional triangulation methods were executable with available data.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $191M to $259M, centered on a median of $225M. The primary uncertainty stems from reliance on a single valuation method; the peer set itself exhibits substantial dispersion (5x spread between min and max), reflecting heterogeneity in business model maturity, customer traction, and capital efficiency across early-stage defense technology companies. The relevance of Series A comparables to a Seed-stage target introduces further methodological tension. Additional data on revenue, customer concentration, or technology differentiation would improve precision.
